open_obligations.what_is_open: Uniform confidence-region coverage and uniform level for the particular numerical directional-delta multiplier construction in \(\textup{def:inference-handle}\), including laws with positive-\(P_X\)-mass endpoint, positive-part, and arm ties. Closing the claim requires a selector-complete, covariate-indexed first-order representation, a uniform conditional multiplier approximation for that representation, and a valid simultaneous grid-to-frontier inversion.
open_obligations.obstruction: The frozen score construction supplies scalar integrated face scores, but the compatibility derivative at a positive-mass tie is function-valued before integration. By \(\textup{lem:scalar-face-tangent-nonfactorization}\), the directions \(r_0=0\) and \(r_1(x)=x_1-1/2\) have the same derivative for every integrated face coordinate while the right directional derivatives of \(C_P(0,0)\) are respectively \(0\) and \(1/4\). Hence the displayed scalar multiplier vector cannot determine the required directional map. The stated envelope and entropy conditions for scalar active scores do not supply the missing covariate-indexed selector process, uniform linearization through support clips and nested ties, conditional covariance and quantile control, or the simultaneous grid-cell inversion. The frozen primitives therefore neither prove nor refute the requested coverage and level guarantees for a suitably completed function-valued procedure.
open_obligations.attempted: The finite maximum representation and the standard convex numerical-delta route were examined, together with cross-fitted orthogonal expansion of each integrated face and tie-retaining contact selection. That route does not close because convexity on the elementary face values does not convert the nonlinear primitive nuisance map, which contains products, into a uniformly linear scalar process, and the scalar active-score class omits the covariate-varying selectors selected before integration. The canonical tangent comparison then gives an explicit nonfactorization certificate, so a scalar-face bootstrap proof cannot be repaired by a sharper remainder estimate alone. A new function-valued construction and its uniform approximation theorem are required.
open_obligations.partial_result: The proved \(\textup{thm:tie-local-separation-obstruction}\) shows that, in the canonical positive-mass endpoint-and-arm-tie subexperiment, the sharp uniform separation order is \(n^{-2\beta/(4\beta+d)}\), while \(\textup{lem:regular-constant-path-power}\) gives the pointwise \(n^{-1/2}\) Gaussian boundary along fixed constant directions. Thus no uniformly level procedure can have a nontrivial worst-case power curve indexed only by \(\sqrt n C_P(\theta)\) over the full tie-inclusive class. The additional \(\textup{lem:scalar-face-tangent-nonfactorization}\) proves that the displayed scalar multiplier input is insufficient to determine the tie derivative: two paths with identical integrated face derivatives have compatibility derivatives \(0\) and \(1/4\). Uniform coverage and uniform null level remain logically possible only after a selector-complete function-valued procedure and its missing uniform approximation and inversion arguments are supplied.
